response.justification: Technically a strong, versatile senior engineer with 15+ years across web, embedded, and open-source systems, plus genuine Linux and open-source depth and founder-level ownership of a Geo-Data platform. However, this is an executive leadership role with hard gates the candidate misses: German is listed at C1 minimum but the candidate self-reports only 'Basic', and the resume shows almost entirely individual-contributor experience with no explicit team-leadership or technical-project-management track record. Python and UML are also absent. A worthwhile conversation only if the German requirement is negotiable and leadership potential can be probed.
strengths: Deep open-source and Linux/Unix background, including founding an open-source platform
Broad, proven software engineering across many domains and languages
Mentoring/teaching signal via workshops, courses, and dojos
concerns: German self-rated 'Basic' vs. required C1 minimum
Predominantly IC roles; no clear team-leadership or project-management experience
No demonstrated UML, Python, or IT security/risk/compliance work
